name: Cacio Pepe Pasta
method: In a large pot, boil salted water. While you are waiting for the water to boil, in a large frying pan on high heat, saute black pepper for a minute. Turn off heat. Boil pasta to very al dente, it’s going to cook an extra minute when you add it to the frying pan. When pasta is ready, turn off heat, don't drain, add pasta with thongs directly to the frying pan. Add half the finely grated cheese, and one ladle of pasta water. Use your discernment when adding pasta water, you want enough to make a creamy sauce, but if you add too much it will become soupy. So, mix, mix, mix that pasta round and round until you start to see the cheese and water form a creamy sauce, add the rest of the cheese and a ladle of pasta water. Mix, mix, mix. Once the sauce is consistent then you can manticare a couple of times before turning it onto a plate. Top with more grated pecorino and cracked pepper. Serving suggestion fresh salad and homemade bread. This is an authentic Roman recipe. Serves 2. Olé
